description:
Introduce an internal `SpecId(u32)` newtype. Convert eight `FnId.0`-keyed codegen structures to `SpecId.0`-indexed. Build the `(FnId, SpecKey) → SpecId` lookup map. Re-run the typer/codegen pipeline so each SpecId has its own Schema, Signature, FuncId, frame_size, parking_reachable membership, and natively_callable membership.

This atom is the structural keying change ONLY. Each fn produces exactly one SpecId (its any-key spec, identical to today's lub-narrowed FnTypes). No narrow specialization is consumed yet — call sites all resolve to the callee's any-key SpecId. CLIF output is byte-identical to the pre-atom baseline.

The follow-up atom (.29.2.1) enables narrow-spec consumption at call sites; that's where new compiled bodies appear and CLIF diffs land.

## Concretely

Eight structures convert:
- `schemas: Vec<Schema>` → SpecId-indexed
- `fn_sigs: Vec<Signature>` → SpecId-indexed
- `fn_ids: HashMap<u32, FuncId>` → `Vec<FuncId>` SpecId-indexed (or `HashMap<SpecId, FuncId>` if churn)
- `fn_ptrs: HashMap<u32, *const u8>` → similar
- `frame_sizes: Vec<u32>` → SpecId-indexed
- `return_descrs: Vec<Descr>` → SpecId-indexed
- `parking_reachable: HashSet<FnId>` → `HashSet<SpecId>`
- `natively_callable: HashSet<FnId>` → `HashSet<SpecId>`

Plus one lookup: `HashMap<(FnId, SpecKey), SpecId>` resolved at call sites.

## SpecId ↔ schema_id

The runtime side keys on `schema_id` (opaque u32). SpecId.0 maps directly to schema_id; SpecIds are dense from 0 so the runtime's `(0..fn_count).map(frame_size_fn)` loop still works without API change.

## Acceptance

- SpecId(u32) defined and plumbed through `compile_with_backend`.
- Eight structures converted; no FnId.0 indexing in codegen except at the callsite-resolution boundary (which under this atom always resolves to the any-key SpecId).
- `cargo test --release` matrix green.
- CLIF byte-identical to pre-atom baseline (verified by `diff -r` across all fixtures).

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.1.

## Blocks

fz-ul4.29.2.1 (narrow-spec consumption).
title: .29.2: SpecId indirection + per-spec codegen
